film.title: Batwara
film.overview: Amidst the fall of princely states in post-independence India, the friendship between Vikram, who comes from an aristocratic family, and a peasant is tested. Vikram turns vengeful after his brother is killed in a peasant colony.

dialogues.dialogue: He said, Brother, the police force needs you. Join the police. So, I took up the job. -Aren't you ashamed of.....yourself to take up a job at the enemy's place? What's the enmity about? His brother had beaten up Lakhan.

What are you saying? Don't forget that Roop sister in law was present over there. I've also come to know that you've done this shameful act.....earlier too. I have the eye witness of the murder of that unborn child. By the way, landlord, they are just..Yes, Hanumant Singh.

By the way sir, If you would address me....as Landlord Hanumant Singh, I would've felt better. Oh! So you're proud to be a landlord than an inspector? Just like that Thakur sir. Well..you can think so.

Hey, get a cup of tea for sir. Hanumant Singh,..you've reached this position from being a constable. ls this something less to be proud of? You're bigger than me on the basis of position. By the way, how's your wound caused by that bullet?
